input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-08T00:58:35.360Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML â€” `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#512BD4, Dark=#7B5FD4}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts automatically when the OS theme changes â€” no event handling needed.

## C# alternative â€” `SetAppTheme`

In code-behind or anywhere you build UI programmatically:

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);

var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty, 
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"), Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E"));
```

`SetAppTheme(property, lightValue, darkValue)` is the extension method equivalent of the XAML markup.

## Scaling with resources

For larger apps, define theme colors once in `App.xaml` so you don't repeat hex values:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">
        <!-- default/light value -->
        #FFFFFF
    </Color>
</Application.Resources>
```

Better yet, use `AppThemeBinding` inside a `Style`:

```xml
<Style TargetType="ContentPage">
    <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}" />
</Style>
```

**Key points:**
- Values update live â€” no restart or manual subscription required.
- Works with any bindable property (colors, images, font sizes, etc.).
- To detect the current theme in C#: `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light` or `AppTheme.Dark`.
